reasoning: The current status of Calicivirus (Norovirus and Sapovirus) vaccine development as of the knowledge cutoff (November 9, 2025) and resolution dates remains negative. Clinical data from November 6, 2025, confirms that Moderna's mRNA-1403 Phase 3 trial—the most advanced candidate—is still accruing cases and will require a second Northern Hemisphere season (winter 2025–26) to gather sufficient efficacy data, meaning results will not be available until at least late 2026. Other major candidates such as HilleVax have discontinued development, and Vaxart's Phase 3 is not expected to start until 2026. Wikipedia's 'List of infectious diseases' correctly reflected this status as 'No' in October 2025. Given the time required for Phase 3 completion, FDA/EMA submission, and approval, it is virtually certain that no vaccine will be listed on Wikipedia by February 2026. Probabilities are set at the floor of 0.05 for the nearest date and increase slightly toward 0.5 for further dates to reflect increasing uncertainty.
